key_insight: Athermal ensemble averaging over local static electronic configurations can reproduce the dynamical self-energy signatures of strongly correlated Mott insulators.
engineering_relevance: This provides a cheaper first-principles route to modeling paramagnetic Mott systems without relying on quantum Monte Carlo or large supercell simulations.
method: Build an on-site dephased ensemble of all accessible local static solutions.
Average local electronic structure thermally to emulate paramagnetic disorder and correlation effects.
Extract frequency-dependent self-energies and local-moment behavior from the ensemble.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports quantitative agreement with DMFT for both self-energy features and persistence of local moments in time.
watch_item: The approach may depend on how completely the local configuration space is sampled and may miss nonlocal correlation effects.
paper_title: Strong correlations and local self-energies from on-site ensembles
